Recommended reading

[Marr, B. (2020) The Intelligence Revolution: Transforming your Business with AI, Kogan Page (Chapter 11)](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2059863) – available on VLeBooks.

Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 8) – available via Kortext.

It is strongly advised that you read the recommended module reading list to ensure you have covered the learning outcomes fully. However, it is not compulsory that you read everything. 

From <[https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-7-enabling-technologies-and-chatgpt-ai-1698/?context_id=15608&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-7-enabling-technologies-and-chatgpt-ai-1698/?context_id=15608&context_resource=lp)>

# Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 8)

[EpubReader](https://r1.vlereader.com/EpubReader?ean=1781805123743)

Can fine tune models. But preparing the datasets can be hard.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Depending on the project, it might be best with prompt engineering, fine tuning, RAG

Cool code locally can train the model on OpenAI

# Main

## Enabling technologies

1. Data collection
2. Data storage -data centres, lakes, Hadoop, Spark, AWS, etc.
3. Data processing and analytics
4. Data output and reporting.

AI as a service

### Journal

Graffiti - GPT api

### Models

The reformer: Reduces the complexity in the attention process and reduces memory requirements

DeBERTa: Makes training more flexible by disentangling content and position.

Hosting models

1. Local
2. Cloud
3. API

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Vision Transformers(ViT)

1. Split the image into patches
2. Linear projection of the flattened images
3. Hybrid embedding transformer encoder

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training(CLiP)

It trains on image pairs and captions.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

## DALL-E

It's a task agnostic model.

Input is a stream of text and images of 1280 tokens, 256 for text and 1014 for the image.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

What model

Does it have an API,

How to access

How much does it cost

Is it a foundation model or is it task specific
